description:
## Background

Workspace switching is an atomic, cancellable transaction. The backend `WorkspaceService` already owns the durable state machine: it validates a candidate, retains a validated catalog entry, loads the Issue Explorer snapshot, and only then commits Current Workspace and MRU state. The renderer receives the same transaction through two asynchronous paths: typed TauRPC responses and `workspace-transition` events.

The atomic-switch slice added a renderer-side guard in `src/App.tsx` to prevent stale or delayed transitions from mixing Workspace identity with Issue Explorer data. It currently coordinates three mutable refs inside `applyTransition`:

- highest accepted generation;
- highest generation that has committed a matching snapshot and is terminal;
- path of the confirmed snapshot currently rendered.

Those refs correctly protect the known event/RPC reorder race: a delayed Pending event for generation N must not replace the workspace state after generation N has committed B's snapshot. The implementation is local and tested, but these values together are an implicit renderer state machine.

## Goal

Make the renderer's transition-admission and snapshot-publication rules an explicit, pure model so future transition sources or phases cannot accidentally reintroduce mixed Workspace/Issue Explorer state. Preserve all behavior and the existing backend transaction contract.

## Why this matters

A Workspace title/path and Issue Explorer snapshot are one visible unit. During Pending or a failed replacement, the old unit remains visible. On a confirmed commit, the new unit replaces it and workspace-scoped detail/search state is reset. A stale, cancelled, or same-generation delayed Pending transition must never regress that unit.

Today the invariants are encoded through ordered checks and independent refs in a React callback. That is acceptable for the current number of paths, but it makes the lifecycle harder to audit and extends tests through `App` even when they are exercising ordering logic rather than UI behavior. A small pure module would improve locality and make invalid transition handling directly testable.

## Proposed shape

Introduce a narrowly scoped renderer transition model (for example `src/workspaces/transition-gate.ts`) with one typed state object and one pure transition function. It should own only admission/presentation decisions, not RPC calls, React state, or backend persistence.

Suggested model inputs:

- current gate state: accepted generation, terminal committed generation, and confirmed snapshot path;
- an incoming `WorkspaceTransition` or typed switch response;
- optional expected generation for a direct RPC response.

Suggested result: a discriminated decision that says one of:

- ignore the payload as stale, mismatched, or terminally superseded;
- accept workspace state while retaining the existing Issue Explorer snapshot;
- commit a matching Issue Explorer snapshot and advance the remount key;
- clear the confirmed snapshot because Current Workspace was removed/reset.

The result must leave React responsible only for applying the decision (`setWorkspaceState`, `setIssueState`, and remount-key updates). Do not turn the backend `WorkspaceState` into a renderer-only type, duplicate backend validation, or add an event bus/reducer framework.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Transition ordering/admission is represented by one typed state object rather than separate lifecycle refs in `App`.
- [ ] A pure unit test covers Pending before success, success before a delayed same-generation Pending, stale older generation, expected-generation mismatch, cancellation/newer selection, and Current removal/reset.
- [ ] The model can distinguish “accept state but retain snapshot”, “commit matching snapshot”, “clear snapshot”, and “ignore” without callers re-deriving those rules.
- [ ] `App` remains the React integration point but no longer owns ordering predicates or lifecycle invariants directly.
- [ ] Existing atomic-switch frontend tests and desktop WebDriver coverage remain green.
- [ ] No behavior changes to the Rust state machine, TauRPC bindings, persistence, retry semantics, or selector presentation.

## Considerations and non-goals

- This is a cleanup, not a new workspace feature. Do not add transition phases merely for abstraction.
- Do not replace the existing backend `WorkspaceService`; it is already the appropriate deep module for durable selection semantics.
- Do not introduce Redux, a global event registry, or a generic state-machine library. A small local discriminated union is preferred if it removes real branches and invalid states.
- Keep the module private to the workspace renderer unless a second caller materializes.
- `WorkspaceTransition` currently infers commit from matching issue data and Current Workspace. Preserve that contract unless a concrete ambiguity requires a typed phase field; adding a transport field solely for theoretical cleanliness is out of scope.
- This can wait until the workspace-management integration work has settled. It should not block `bsm-kia.5`, because the existing behavior is covered and correct.

## Likely files

- `src/App.tsx` — delegate admission decisions to the model and apply returned effects.
- `src/workspaces/transition-gate.ts` (new) — pure typed state and decision function.
- `src/workspaces/transition-gate.test.ts` (new) — ordering and snapshot-invariant matrix.
- `src/App.test.tsx` — retain only integration assertions that prove React applies decisions correctly.

## Validation

- `pnpm test`
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`
- `pnpm run check` (record the known unrelated ADR formatting failure separately if it remains)
- Run the focused workspace-switch WebDriver suite if the `App` integration changes materially.

## Rationale for deferring

The current implementation is boring, local, and correct; the three refs do not justify a framework. The cleanup becomes worthwhile because they already encode a non-trivial ordering protocol across event and RPC delivery. Doing it as a focused follow-up avoids mixing a presentation-model refactor with the completed atomic-switch behavior and desktop acceptance work.
title: Model renderer workspace transitions explicitly
